text: Working with APIs allows applications to communicate and share data. You interact with APIs by making HTTP requests, such as GET to retrieve data or POST to send it. The response is usually in JSON format, which is easy to read and use. Many APIs require authentication, often using an API key, to control access. You must also be aware of rate limits, which restrict how many requests you can make in a certain time. Finally, good error handling is important—you should always check for error codes and handle them gracefully in your code.
